title: eriogonum umbellatum var. ramulosum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. ramulosum, also known as sulfur buckwheat or Utah buckwheat, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. Eriogonum umbellatum var. ramulosum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve for topical use, and its leaves can be dried and used as a medicinal tea.
